_comment: Per-NEW-crate cargo-public-api baseline per seq-minor-5 + gap #1a (r2-test-landscape §5). The F2 test `ipc_method_name_stability_drift_detector.rs` references this file as the source of truth for the IPC method-name allowlist; the `_anticipated_method_set` array is matched byte-for-byte against `TauriRenderer::ipc_method_allowlist()` at HEAD. Adding a method requires an explicit baseline update + admin UI v0 manifest review (T3 defense — silent IPC surface expansion is a manifest-bypass risk).
_surfaces: pub struct IpcRequest { pub method: String, pub payload: serde_json::Value, pub session: Option<SessionToken> }
pub struct IpcResponse { pub payload: serde_json::Value }
pub enum IpcError { MethodNotInAllowlist, CapabilityNotInManifest, SessionResolve(ThinClientSessionError), MissingSession }
pub struct AdminUiManifest { pub granted_caps: BTreeSet<String> }
pub fn AdminUiManifest::with_caps<I, S>(caps: I) -> Self
pub fn InProcessSessionBridge::resolve(&self, token: &SessionToken, presented_origin: &str) -> Result<String, ThinClientSessionError>
pub fn TauriRenderer::with_bridge(self, bridge: InProcessSessionBridge) -> Self
pub fn TauriRenderer::dispatch_ipc(&self, request: IpcRequest) -> Result<IpcResponse, IpcError>

_ipc_method_name_allowlist_baseline._comment: Drift-detector baseline for the IPC allowlist method-name set, per gap #1a. The drift-detector pin asserts the populated list at HEAD matches this baseline byte-for-byte. Adding a method requires an explicit baseline update + manifest review (T3 defense — silent IPC surface expansion is a manifest-bypass risk).
